name: Application Whitelisting Bypass via Dxcap Detected
description: Detects adversaries bypassing process and/or signature-based defenses by executing `Dxcap.exe`. This activity is often used for application whitelisting bypass via trusted developer utilities proxy execution. The detection focuses on command-line arguments that indicate command execution, which is highly suspicious and requires immediate attention.

prompts.prompt: A critical alert has been triggered on the host **{computer_name}**: the process `dxcap.exe` executed the command line **{command_line}**, which contains arguments indicative of **Trusted Developer Utilities Proxy Execution** (**MITRE ATT&CK T1127**). This technique is a known method for bypassing application whitelisting and executing arbitrary code. The parent process was **{parent_image}** and the user was **{user}**. While `dxcap.exe` is a legitimate tool, its use with these parameters is highly suspicious. Immediate investigation is required to determine the nature of the child process being launched and whether this is a legitimate developer activity or a malicious attack.

parameters.name: command_line
parameters.description: The full command line that includes '-c' and a reference to an executable.

parameters.name: parent_image
parameters.description: The parent process that launched dxcap.exe.

parameters.name: user
parameters.description: The user account under which the process ran.

parameters.name: computer_name
parameters.description: The name of the computer where the event occurred.
